driver_rates.name: 10-Year Treasury Yield
driver_rates.explanation: Benchmark for 30-year fixed mortgage pricing. When the 10Y rises, mortgages follow within days.

driver_rates.name: 30-Year Treasury Yield
driver_rates.explanation: Long-end of the curve. Direct sentiment proxy for very long-duration debt like mortgage-backed bonds.

driver_rates.name: Federal Funds Rate
driver_rates.explanation: The Fed's policy rate. Sets the floor for short-term borrowing and bleeds into mortgage pricing via expectations.

driver_rates.name: SOFR (Overnight Funding)
driver_rates.explanation: Successor to LIBOR. Indexes most new ARMs and floating-rate commercial mortgages.

driver_rates.name: MBS Current-Coupon Spread
driver_rates.explanation: 30-yr mortgage minus 10-yr Treasury. The mortgage industry's own risk premium — wider spread = costlier mortgages.

related_indicators.name: S&P 500
related_indicators.explanation: Risk-on equity rallies pull capital out of bonds and lift mortgage rates.

related_indicators.name: CPI YoY
related_indicators.explanation: Headline inflation. Hotter prints push the Fed hawkish and lift the long end of the curve.

related_indicators.name: Core PCE YoY
related_indicators.explanation: The Fed's preferred inflation gauge — drives policy expectations more than CPI.

related_indicators.name: Unemployment Rate
related_indicators.explanation: Labor slack. Higher unemployment usually means lower mortgage rates as Treasury yields fall.

related_indicators.name: Real GDP YoY
related_indicators.explanation: Overall growth. Strong growth + tight labor = sticky rates.

related_indicators.name: Housing Starts (SAAR, k)
related_indicators.explanation: Supply pipeline. Affects home prices more than rates, but feeds Fed thinking on shelter inflation.

related_indicators.name: Existing Home Sales (SAAR, k)
related_indicators.explanation: Demand-side housing health — declines often precede rate cuts.

key_indicators.name: 10Y–2Y Yield Spread
key_indicators.explanation: Difference between the 10-year and 2-year Treasury yields. A negative spread (inverted curve) has preceded most U.S. recessions.

key_indicators.name: Trade-Weighted Dollar Index
key_indicators.explanation: Broad measure of the dollar vs. major trading partners. A strong dollar imports disinflation and tends to cap U.S. rates.

key_indicators.name: VIX Volatility Index
key_indicators.explanation: S&P 500 30-day implied volatility. Spikes coincide with flight-to-quality bond buying, which pulls mortgage rates lower.

key_indicators.name: Consumer Sentiment
key_indicators.explanation: University of Michigan survey. Forward-looking proxy for spending, housing demand, and inflation expectations.

key_indicators.name: M2 Money Stock ($B)
key_indicators.explanation: Broad money supply. The liquidity backdrop for everything — outsized expansion fed the 2020–2022 inflation surge.
